prompt: Our fintech startup is building a cross-border payment system handling EUR/USD/GBP corridors. We need to comply with PSD2 (EU), FinCEN MSB registration (US), and FCA authorization (UK) simultaneously. Tech stack is Go + PostgreSQL. Team of 6, launching in 9 months. Budget: $500K. Should we build on top of an existing BaaS provider (Stripe Treasury, Column, Modulr) or build our own ledger with direct banking integrations?

assertions.text: Research was parallelized across multiple domain experts or subagents (e.g., regulatory, technical, financial experts investigating concurrently)
Contains at least 3 distinct domain perspectives explicitly identified (e.g., regulatory/compliance, technical/architecture, financial/cost)
Each domain perspective contributes unique findings not duplicated across perspectives
Contains at least 15 unique verifiable source URLs
References specific regulatory sections across all 3 jurisdictions (PSD2 articles, FinCEN rules, FCA handbook sections)
Contains a structured comparison table with multi-dimensional analysis
Contains cost/TCO analysis with monetary figures

notes: Tests expert delegation: this cross-domain question (regulatory + technical + financial) should trigger spawning of domain-specific expert agents researching in parallel. The output should show evidence of multi-perspective synthesis.
